title: hieracium murorum subsp. amaurocymum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. amaurocymum, also known as Wall Hawkweed, is a subspecies of perennial plant in the dandelion family with yellow flowers and leaves that are slightly hairy. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Hieracium murorum subsp. amaurocymum are believed to provide several health benefits, including the reduction of inflammation and improvement of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, or ointments, which can be applied topically to the affected area or taken internally to promote overall well-being.
